sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 76, level 2.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 25 of about 50 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After RIGHT 6:
- Dark gray (c): pixels=36, bbox=[18,57]-[26,62] - now a single piece again, moved further right
- Black (B): still at [18,45]-[29,59], 64 pixels
- The dark gray is now at cols 57-62, which is to the right of the black character at cols 45-59
The dark gray character has moved past the black character. Looking at the image, the dark gray is now on the far right side, partially overlapping with the black character.
Let me think about what the goal might be. The dark gray character started on the left side of the divider, and I've been moving it right. It crossed the divider and is now on the right side, past the black character.
Maybe the goal is to have the dark gray character match the black character's shape/position exactly. Let me check the hashes:
- Black: hash=23e5ac53ba050c61
- Dark gray: hash=dd337f230da29cff
They have different hashes, so they're different shapes.
Wait, let me reconsider. In Level 1, the goal was to move the dark gray character to the yellow L-shape's position. The yellow L-shape was the target. In Level 2, maybe the goal is still to move the dark gray character to the yellow L-shape's position, but the mechanics are different.
The yellow L-shape is at [42,3]-[53,17], which is at the bottom left. The dark gray character is now at [18,57]-[26,62], which is at the top right. I can't move vertically, so I can't reach the yellow L-shape.
Unless... the goal is something else entirely. Maybe the goal is to have the dark gray character overlap with the black character in a specific way, or to have them form a specific pattern.
Let me try a different approach. Let me undo all the RIGHTs and try moving LEFT instead. Maybe the goal is to move the dark gray character to the left, toward the yellow L-shape.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the movement mechanics. In Level 1, LEFT moved the dark gray character to the left (toward the yellow L-shape). In Level 2, LEFT moves the dark gray character to the left by 6 cols per action.
The dark gray character is at [18,15]-[29,29] (original position). The yellow L-shape is at [42,3]-[53,17]. To reach the yellow L-shape's column range [3,17], I need to move left by 15-3=12 cols (from the left edge of dark gray to the left edge of yellow L). That's 12/6 = 2 LEFT actions.
But the yellow L-shape is at rows 42-53, and the dark gray is at rows 18-29. I can't move vertically, so I can't reach the yellow L-shape's row range.
Hmm, maybe the goal is not to reach the yellow L-shape's exact position, but to have the dark gray character's column range match the yellow L-shape's column range. Let me try LEFT 2 to see if the dark gray character's columns match the yellow L-shape's columns.
Actually, let me first undo all the RIGHTs to get back to the starting position.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: Dark gray now past black character. Need to rethink. Let me undo all RIGHTs and try LEFT toward yellow L-shape instead.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Undo 6 RIGHTs with 6 LEFTs to get back to start
action(['LEFT']*6)

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After undo:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 10 and n['color'] in ['c', 'B', 'Y']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {n['color']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
